evidence.scientific_clarity: Forest plot clearly shows 12 study CIs with inverse-variance-weighted squares, diamond pooled estimate, and null-effect reference line — brief requirements fully met.
evidence.typography: No fonttype=42 set in script; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap-rasterized) DejaVuSans — not editable/selectable; font is the Matplotlib default DejaVu Sans.
evidence.palette: CI lines and squares use a single blue (#1f77b4) and pooled diamond uses red (#d62728); colorblind-safe for dichromats but red diamond on a directional-neutral summary is a minor convention issue.
evidence.layout_density: Panel fills well for 12 studies; pooled annotation text at right edge may clip on narrow layouts; y-range is sensibly tight but right x-limit (1.1) leaves modest empty space.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: No legend (colors unexplained in plot body); pooled annotation is redundant with caption; caption accurately describes the figure; overall close to publication-ready.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses Type 3 fonts (bitmap-embedded), meaning text is not selectable or editable; script lacks 'matplotlib.rcParams["pdf.fonttype"] = 42'.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names all key elements (squares, CIs, diamond, dashed line), explains inverse-variance weighting, and provides the specific pooled estimate with 95% CI numerical values.

overall_strengths: Caption is precise and informative, providing the pooled estimate value and CI, and explaining the weighting methodology.
All required output files are present (PDF, PNG, SVG, caption.tex, gen_figure.py) and the figure correctly implements the forest-plot design from the brief.
overall_weaknesses: Font rendering is non-vector (Type 3 / bitmap) because fonttype=42 was never set, making text in the PDF non-selectable and unsuitable for journal submission.
Default DejaVu Sans font is used with no explicit rcParams override; the figure lacks an in-plot legend explaining marker colors, which a reviewer would likely flag.
